ocr.text: In the study of DAMP, various disease models illustrate the effects of deletion. For Biglycan, renal inflammation (unilateral
ureteral obstruction) showed reduced kidney damage, as referenced in [87]. In the sepsis model using LPS or zymosan, deletion
provided protection from lethality, referenced in [23, 87]. FNEDA demonstrated reduced lesion size, number, and macrophage
infiltration in atherosclerosis (Apo E deficient, high fat diet), as noted in [139]. MRP8/MRP14 indicated reduced bacterial
dissemination, systemic inflammation, and liver damage during abdominal sepsis (E. coli), with reference [140]. Furthermore,
arterial injury (femoral wire insertion) revealed reduced inflammatory cell infiltration and neointimal formation, referenced in
[141]. Atherosclerosis (Apo E deficient, high fat diet) was also associated with attenuated atherosclerotic lesions and
macrophage accumulation in plaques, as mentioned in [141].
DAMP  Disease Model  Effect of deletion  Refs
Biglycan  Renal inflammation (unilateral ureteral obstruction)  Reduced kidney damage  [87]
Sepsis (LPS or zymosan)  Protected from lethality  [23, 87]
FNEDA  Atherosclerosis (Apo E deficient, high fat diet)  Reduced lesion size, number and macrophage infiltration  [139]
MRP8/MRP14  Abdominal sepsis (E. coli)  Reduced bacterial dissemination, systemic inflammation, liver damage  [140]
Arterial injury (femoral wire insertion)  Reduced inflammatory cell infiltration and neotintimal formation  [141]
Atherosclerosis (Apo E deficient, high fat diet)  Attenuated atherosclerotic lesions and macrophage accumulation in plaques  [141]
Cerebral ischemia (cerebral artery occlusion)  Reduced lesion volume, brain swelling and inflammatory cell infiltration  [142]
Lethal sepsis (LPS )  Protects from lethality  [143]
Vasculitis (cytokine induced)  Reduced neutrophil accumulation and lesion severity  [141]
Tenascin-C  Destructive joint inflammation (antigen induced arthritis)  Protected from joint erosion and tissue destruction  [24]
Tumorigenesis (cross with MMTV/PyV mice)  Induced smaller tumor nests  [144]
In cerebral ischemia (cerebral artery occlusion), there was reduced lesion volume, brain swelling, and inflammatory cell
infiltration, noted in [142]. Lethal sepsis (LPS) provided evidence that deletion protects from lethality, referenced in [143].
Vasculitis (cytokine induced) showed reduced neutrophil accumulation and lesion severity, as per [141]. Tenascin-C in
destructive joint inflammation (antigen induced arthritis) was found to protect from joint erosion and tissue destruction,
referenced in [24]. Finally, tumorigenesis, when crossed with MMTV/PyV mice, induced smaller tumor nests, as noted in [144].
